Class MLDetector

java.lang.Object
com.semanticprivacyguard.detector.MLDetector
All Implemented Interfaces:
PIIDetector
public final class MLDetector extends Object implements PIIDetector
Layer 2 detector — context-aware classification using a Naive Bayes model.

How it disambiguates

The regex layer catches structurally obvious PII (credit cards, SSNs, emails). But many tokens — especially proper nouns — are ambiguous:

  • "Apple" in "I ate an Apple" → fruit, benign.
  • "Apple" in "Contact Apple at 555-0199" → company PII.
  • "John" in "the Gospel of John" → biblical reference.
  • "John" in "Dear John, your SSN is…" → person's name.

The ML detector scans every title-cased token (potential name or org) and every high-entropy token (potential key or secret), extracts a context feature vector via FeatureExtractor, and asks the Naive Bayes classifier whether the context is PII-like or benign.

Confidence threshold

A token is flagged only if the "PII" class posterior probability exceeds the configurable confidenceThreshold (default 0.65). Callers can tighten (more precise) or loosen (more recall-oriented) this threshold via the SPGConfig builder.

Since:
1.0.0
Author:
Hemant Naik

      Creates a detector using the built-in training corpus and default threshold.

    • MLDetector

      public MLDetector(NaiveBayesClassifier classifier, FeatureExtractor featureExtractor, double confidenceThreshold)
      Creates a detector with a custom classifier and threshold.
      Parameters:
      classifier - a pre-fitted Naive Bayes classifier
      featureExtractor - feature extraction strategy
      confidenceThreshold - PII posterior probability cutoff in (0, 1]

  • Method Details

    • name

      public String name()
      Description copied from interface: PIIDetector
      Returns a short human-readable name used in logging and metrics (e.g. "HeuristicDetector", "NaiveBayesMLDetector").
      Specified by:
      name in interface PIIDetector

    • detect

      public List<PIIMatch> detect(String text)
      Description copied from interface: PIIDetector
      Scans the supplied text and returns every PII span detected.

      Matches may overlap; callers (typically CompositeDetector) are responsible for de-duplication.

      Specified by:
      detect in interface PIIDetector
      Parameters:
      text - the text to scan (never null)
      Returns:
      mutable list of detected spans; empty if none found
